bottom_tab_bar.style: Border-top with subtle shadow, slightly taller for modern feel, icon + label.

contextual_widget.style: Large rounded card (rounded-3xl), generous padding (p-8). Used for 'Next Appointment' or 'Welcome Back'.

quick_action_button.style: Square or squircle (rounded-2xl) card, icon on top, text below. Drop soft shadow. Text should be small, centered.

appointment_card.style: Row layout with Doctor Avatar (rounded-full), Name, Speciality, Time/Date badge. Primary action button 'Join Video' or 'Reschedule'.

promo_banner.style: Horizontal scrollable card, Image background with subtle dark overlay to keep text legible. rounded-2xl.

promo_banners.description: Abstract organic pattern for banner background

instructions_to_main_agent: Since this is a React Native Expo application, strictly use React Native primitives (View, Text, ScrollView, TouchableOpacity, Image) or NativeWind for styling. Do NOT use HTML elements like div, span, or section.
Navigation must be implemented using React Navigation (e.g., @react-navigation/bottom-tabs and @react-navigation/native-stack).
Implement bottom tabs: Home | My Appointments | Health Record | My Account.
For the QR Code feature in 'Digital Prescription', use a library like 'react-native-qrcode-svg' instead of an image if possible. Otherwise, construct a dummy UI with a mocked square barcode.
Include 'testID' attributes (the React Native equivalent of data-testid) for all interactive elements to support testing.
The UI should look calm, organic, and trustworthy. Use generous paddings (`p-6` on containers) and the specified warm color palette.
The Home dashboard should have dynamic widgets. E.g., if there's an upcoming appointment, show the dark green (#2A433A) contextual widget at the top.
No real authentication is needed, build a mock flow or just a simulated splash/welcome screen that automatically navigates to the Home tab.
Ensure 'Outfit' and 'Manrope' fonts are loaded via Expo Font, or fallback to system sans-serif if you cannot load custom fonts.
